results.title: Whisper API Pricing (2026) — $0.006/min vs Self‑Host Costs
excerpts:
Section Title: Whisper API Pricing (2026) — $0.006/min vs Self‑Host Costs
Content:
The Whisper API eliminates infrastructure—no servers, no GPUs, no scaling headaches. Just $0.006/min and an API call. But you lose control over model versions, processing customization, and data residency. And for high-volume deployments (500+ hours/month), API costs can exceed self-hosted infrastructure expenses.
For comparing transcription pricing across all major services, see our comprehensive cost analysis.
In this guide, we'll break down OpenAI Whisper API's complete 2026 pricing, calculate the true cost of self-hosting Whisper (infrastructure + DevOps + maintenance), reveal when each approach makes sense, and show you a simpler $6.00 flat rate alternative that requires neither API integration nor infrastructure management.
**At a glance:** API: $0.006/min • Self-host: $276+/mo • BrassTranscripts: $6 flat
Section Title: Whisper API Pricing (2026) — $0.006/min vs Self‑Host Costs
Content:
**Quick answer:** Managed Whisper API = $0.006/min; self‑host costs start at $276/mo — use our calculator to see which is cheaper for your volume.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Per-minute pricing, speaker ID, and additional fees
Content:
| Service | Price Per Minute | Price Per Hour | Model |
| **Whisper API** | **$0.006/min** | $0.36/hour | Whisper large-v2 |
| **GPT-4o Transcribe** | $0.006/min | $0.36/hour | GPT-4o audio |
| **GPT-4o Mini Transcribe** | **$0.003/min** | $0.18/hour | GPT-4o mini audio |
 ... 
Section Title: Whisper API Pricing (2026) — $0.006/min vs Self‑Host Costs > ... > Billing Details
Content:
**Per-minute billing** rounded to nearest second
Charges based on actual audio duration
No minimum file size
25 MB file size limit per request
Supports 50+ languages
Section Title: Whisper API Pricing (2026) — $0.006/min vs Self‑Host Costs > ... > Free Tier
Content:
**OpenAI does NOT offer a free tier for Whisper API.** You need:
OpenAI account (free to create)
API key
Credit card on file
Pay-as-you-go from first minute
**Comparison to competitors:** Many competitors offer free credits or trial tiers for new users. OpenAI Whisper API has **no free tier** —you pay from the first minute. Check each provider's current offers before committing.
This means testing costs real money—even a 10-hour proof-of-concept costs $3.60.

Content:
November 3, 2025 · 12 min read
Finding the best speech to text API means comparing pricing, accuracy, setup time, and infrastructure. This speech to text API comparison covers five leading providers with real code examples and honest tradeoffs to help you choose the best speech recognition API for your use case.
Section Title: ... > Best Speech to Text API Comparison ​

| Provider | Setup Time | Pricing | Key Strength |
| **Google Cloud Speech-to-Text** | 30-45 min | $0.016-$0.024/min | 125+ languages |
| **AWS Transcribe** | 30-45 min | $0.024/min | Real-time streaming |
| **Azure Speech** | 30-45 min | $0.017/min | Pronunciation assessment |
| **OpenAI Whisper API** | 15-20 min | $0.006/min | Best accuracy |
| **VocaFuse** | <15 min | Contact | Production infrastructure included |

Home Blog ElevenLabs Pricing (2026): Pla...
 ... 
Section Title: ElevenLabs Pricing (2026): Plans, Credits, Commercial Rights, and API Costs
Content:
Jessica Becker • Mar 24, 2026 • 8 min read
ElevenLabs has established itself as the leading AI voice generation platform, powering text-to-speech, voice cloning, and conversational AI agents for creators, developers, and enterprises. But the pricing structure — with separate UI and API plans, a credit system tied to character counts, and commercial rights that only kick in on paid tiers — can be confusing to navigate.
This guide breaks down every ElevenLabs plan as of early 2026: what each tier costs, how credits work, where commercial rights begin, how API pricing differs from the standard UI subscription, and who each plan is designed for. We also cover how BIGVU's built-in voiceover and caption tools compare for video creators who need voice capabilities without a standalone voice platform subscription.
 ... 
Section Title: ElevenLabs Pricing (2026): Plans, Credits, Commercial Rights, and API Costs > ... > Free Plan
Content:
ElevenLabs' free tier provides 10,000 credits per month, which translates to roughly 10 minutes of high-quality text-to-speech using the Multilingual v2 model, or approximately 15 minutes of Conversational AI agent time. The free plan does not include commercial usage rights — you must attribute ElevenLabs in any public content. You cannot legally monetize content created on the free plan. Instant voice cloning is not available, and you're limited to the stock voice library.
Section Title: ... > Starter Plan ($5/month)
Content:
Starter is the entry point for commercial use. It provides 30,000 credits per month (~30 minutes of TTS), commercial licensing rights, and access to instant voice cloning. This is the minimum tier for YouTubers, podcasters, or marketers who want to use ElevenLabs output in monetized content. Studio and Dubbing API access is included.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Scale Plan ($330/month)
Content:
Scale includes 2,000,000 credits (~2,000 minutes of TTS), multi-seat workspaces, and low-latency TTS optimized for real-time applications. Designed for product teams building voice features into their applications.
Section Title: ... > Business Plan ($1,320/month)
Content:
Business provides 11,000,000 credits (~11,000 minutes of TTS), multi-seat workspaces, and professional voice clone capability across the organization. Enterprise-level features with standard billing.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > API Pricing Is Separate
Content:
ElevenLabs maintains separate API subscription tiers that are independent from the standard UI plans. The API Free tier includes 10 credits per month. API Pro costs $99/month with 100 credits. API Scale is $330/month with 660 credits. If you're a developer building voice features into an application, you need an API subscription in addition to (or instead of) a standard plan. Speech-to-text via the UI costs more credits than through the API. Plan carefully based on whether you'll use the web interface, the API, or both.

results.title: Cost Analysis - SFlow
excerpts:
Section Title: Cost Analysis > Overview
Content:
SFlow uses [Groq's Whisper API](https://console.groq.com/docs/speech-to-text) for speech-to-text transcription. Groq charges **$0.02 per hour** of audio transcribed. This page breaks down real-world costs and compares them to Wispr Flow's $15/month subscription.
<Info>
Groq offers a **free tier** with generous limits for personal use. Most users won't need to pay anything for months.
</Info>
Section Title: Cost Analysis > Groq Pricing > Current Rates (as of 2024)
Content:
| Model | Cost per Hour | Cost per Minute | Cost per Second |
| **whisper-large-v3-turbo** | $0.02 | $0.000333 | $0.0000056 |
Section Title: Cost Analysis > Groq Pricing > Current Rates (as of 2024)
Content:
SFlow uses **whisper-large-v3-turbo** by default (fastest model with highest accuracy).
 ... 
Section Title: Cost Analysis > Frequently Asked Questions
Content:
<AccordionGroup>
<Accordion title="What if I exceed the free tier?" icon="circle-question">
Groq will prompt you to add a credit card. You'll then pay $0.02/hour for any audio transcribed beyond the free tier limits. For most users, this is still **$1-5/month maximum**.
</Accordion>
<Accordion title="Are there any hidden fees?" icon="circle-question">
No. Groq charges **only** for audio transcription time. No monthly minimums, no platform fees, no surprise charges.
</Accordion>
<Accordion title="What if Groq raises prices?" icon="circle-question">
SFlow is open-source. You can:
```
1. Switch to a different Whisper API provider (OpenAI, Replicate, etc.)
2. Run Whisper locally (free but slower)
3. Fork the code and use any STT service

No vendor lock-in!
```
</Accordion>
<Accordion title="How does this compare to OpenAI Whisper?" icon="circle-question">
OpenAI charges **$0.006/minute** ($0.36/hour) — **18x more expensive** than Groq.
Section Title: Cost Analysis > Frequently Asked Questions
Content:
```
| Provider | Cost per Hour | 30 hours/year |
| -------- | ------------- | ------------- |
| Groq     | \$0.02        | **\$0.60**    |
| OpenAI   | \$0.36        | **\$10.80**   |

Groq uses the same Whisper model but with optimized hardware (faster + cheaper).
```
</Accordion>
<Accordion title="Can I use SFlow offline?" icon="circle-question">
Not currently. SFlow requires internet to call Groq's API. However, you can:
```
* Run Whisper locally using `whisper` Python package (free, slower)
* Modify `core/transcriber.py` to use a local model

Local transcription removes API costs entirely but requires a powerful CPU/GPU.
```
</Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

Best TTS APIs in 2026: ElevenLabs, Google, AWS & 9...
Section Title: Best TTS APIs in 2026: ElevenLabs, Google, AWS & 9 More Compared
Content:
From ultra-fast conversational AI to studio-quality narration, compare 12 text-to-speech APIs — including ElevenLabs, Google Cloud, Amazon Polly and Speechmatics — to find the voice that matches your use case and budget.
Tom Young Digital Specialist
[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tom-young-01/)
The text-to-speech market is currently very loud and very human.
What was once the domain of robotic, stilted voices has transformed into [a competitive landscape](https://artificialanalysis.ai/text-to-speech/models) of neural networks producing uncannily human speech.
Having just released our own natural, low-latency text-to-speech API (at a fraction of the cost of established vendors like ElevenLabs, Amazon Polly and Google Cloud TTS), we thought it worth sharing our findings on what's out there.
 ... 
Section Title: Best TTS APIs in 2026: ElevenLabs, Google, AWS & 9 More Compared > The contenders: Who's who in TTS
Content:
Below, we profile the best TTS APIs available to builders and developers today, examining which services excel for specific scenarios and where the real value lies beyond marketing hype.
 ... 
Section Title: Best TTS APIs in 2026: ElevenLabs, Google, AWS & 9 More Compared > The verdict: Horses for courses
Content:
But if you're deploying voice agents at scale: support calls, outbound sales, real-time announcements, IVR systems. The calculus shifts. You don't need voices that laugh, scream, or cry on command. You need clarity, responsiveness, and costs that don't spiral as you grow.
This is where purpose-built, focused players like Speechmatics were built.
Sub-150ms latency that holds in production (not just demos), genuinely affordable pricing ($0.011 per 1,000 characters - up to 27x cheaper than ElevenLabs), and natural-sounding voices without the feature bloat.
It's TTS built for the real world, not the pitch deck. When you're processing millions of calls or conversations monthly, that price difference isn't academic. It's the difference between a viable business model and burning cash on features your customers will never notice.
Section Title: Best TTS APIs in 2026: ElevenLabs, Google, AWS & 9 More Compared > The verdict: Horses for courses
Content:
The market has matured beyond "can a machine sound human?" to "which machine sounds human in the way my application needs, at a price I can sustain?" The answer is rarely one-size-fits-all. Test the free tiers, benchmark the latency in production conditions (not just demos), and scrutinize the pricing structure against your projected volume. Your use case, and budget, will dictate the winner.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > What text-to-speech app is free?
Content:
Many TTS APIs offer free tiers. Google Cloud TTS provides 1 million characters per month free for WaveNet voices. Amazon Polly offers 5 million characters per month free for the first year (standard voices). Microsoft Azure includes 500,000 characters per month free for neural voices. ElevenLabs has a free tier with approximately 10,000 characters per month. Beyond cloud APIs, there are standalone apps and services: Natural Reader, Balabolka, and TTSReader offer free versions with limitations. For developers, open-source options like Coqui TTS or Mozilla TTS can be self-hosted for free, though you'll need to handle infrastructure.
 ...

results.title: The Complete Guide to ElevenLabs Plans Overages and Usage Based Pricing in 2026 | Flexprice
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > The Complete Guide to ElevenLabs Plans Overages and Usage Based Pricing in 2026
Content:
Jan 22, 2026
Jan 22, 2026
• 10 min read
• 10 min read
Aanchal Parmar
Product Marketing Manager, Flexprice
[ElevenLabs](https://elevenlabs.io/) is a leading AI audio platform known for its lifelike voice generation, real-time cloning, and multilingual dubbing. Whether you're a solo creator or an enterprise team, it offers the infrastructure to generate and scale voice-based content.
In this guide, we will break down the full pricing model: plan comparisons, overage logic, voice model differences, and how to choose the right tier (or replicate this pricing system for your own SaaS).
 ... 
Section Title: ... > How ElevenLabs Prices Across Models and Features
Content:
ElevenLabs doesn’t just charge by output it charges by **model type** and [**feature usage**](https://flexprice.io/blog/best-tools-for-usage-analytics-and-billing-in-2025) . Each product category like Text-to-Speech or Dubbing has its own pricing logic based on characters, minutes, or hours. And within each, there are **two pricing levers** :
→ Included quota (per plan)
→ Overage cost (per unit after quota)
Here’s how pricing breaks down by model and feature:
 ... 
Section Title: ... > What happens when you exceed your usage plan?
Content:
Each ElevenLabs plan comes with fixed monthly quotas across various features like text-to-speech, speech-to-text, voice tools, dubbing, and more. Once you exceed those included limits, you’re charged per unit of extra usage and this overage pricing is tiered. The higher your plan, the lower your per-unit costs, which is ElevenLabs’ way of rewarding scale and nudging upgrades.
Let’s break that down by feature:
 ... 
Section Title: ... > 4. Speech-to-Text (API-based transcription)
Content:
Creator includes around 63 hours, Pro includes 300 hours, Scale includes 1,100 hours, and Business gives 6,000 hours.
Overage rates start at $0.40/hour on Creator and Pro, drop to $0.30 on Scale, and go as low as $0.22/hour on Business.
 ... 
Section Title: The Complete Guide to ElevenLabs Plans Overages and Usage Based Pricing in 2026 > ... > 6. Dubbing:
Content:
Included quotas for dubbing are 50 minutes on Creator, 250 on Pro, 1,000 on Scale, and 5,500 on Business. Overage rates again follow a descending pattern from $0.60/min on lower plans to $0.24/min on higher tiers.
